title: We Finally Weld The Van For You
author: Sponsored
snippet: The A-Team burns through $40k of welding supplies per episode. Our 14-DVD course teaches you how to weaponize scrap metal on a budget — without the montage music.
full_text:
The A-Team burns through $40k of welding supplies per episode. Our 14-DVD course teaches you how to weaponize scrap metal on a budget — without the montage music.

Includes: the Murdock Unit (flight physics), the Hannibal Module (cigar-chewing as morale engine), and our signature 30-second cabbage cannon. No previous combat experience required. Legally distinct from the A-Team in all 50 states.

title: Watched it last night with my dad. Nothing else on TV is this fun.
author: Bobby K.
snippet: Mr. T threw a guy through a corrugated roof and the guy got up and dusted himself off. My dad laughed so hard he spilled his beer. What else do you want from television?
full_text:
Mr. T threw a guy through a corrugated roof and the guy got up and dusted himself off. My dad laughed so hard he spilled his beer. What else do you want from television?

The van is the best vehicle in history. Face is handsome. Murdock is nuts. Hannibal has the cigar. It's not complicated. Everyone gets a specialty and they use it. At the end the bad guys lose and nobody actually dies. I don't know why that's supposed to be dumb.

title: A disposable artifact of Reagan-era television pablum
author: Harold Finnerty
snippet: Every episode is the same episode. Four men drive to a farm, get locked in a shed, weld a cabbage cannon, and liberate a Midwestern widow. This is not storytelling. It is a checklist with stunt work.
full_text:
Every episode is the same episode. Four men drive to a farm, get locked in a shed, weld a cabbage cannon, and liberate a Midwestern widow. This is not storytelling. It is a checklist with stunt work.

Stephen J. Cannell has better shows in his catalogue and better shows will come. The A-Team will not survive syndication. I put its shelf life at three years past cancellation. Children watching it now will remember the van and forget everything else. And frankly, that is all the show deserves.

title: I rewatched the whole series in one summer and I cannot defend it but I love it
author: Marcy Osawa
snippet: The plots are interchangeable. The action physics are impossible. Nobody ever gets hurt. And it is somehow the most honest show I have ever seen about friendship between four men who have nothing in common except a skill set.
full_text:
The plots are interchangeable. The action physics are impossible. Nobody ever gets hurt. And it is somehow the most honest show I have ever seen about friendship between four men who have nothing in common except a skill set.

I think what my 1987 self missed is that the dumbness is load-bearing. You cannot tell a story about men who genuinely like each other on 1980s network TV unless you dress it up as an action show. The welding scenes ARE the emotional scenes. When Hannibal says 'I love it when a plan comes together,' he is saying 'I love these idiots.'

title: I became a director because of that van
author: Benicio Ruiz
snippet: I was seven. I watched the van hit a ramp and time stopped. I did not know yet that editing existed, or that the ramp was built by grips, or that B.A. Baracus was an actor with a stage name. I just knew I wanted to make the ramp.
full_text:
I was seven. I watched the van hit a ramp and time stopped. I did not know yet that editing existed, or that the ramp was built by grips, or that B.A. Baracus was an actor with a stage name. I just knew I wanted to make the ramp.

The A-Team is the purest expression of a single idea: that a good plan and four friends can solve anything in 47 minutes. Everything I make is in conversation with that idea. The 2010 movie missed it completely. The show understood that the real special effect was Hannibal's face when the plan worked.

title: Semiotically speaking, it is a Western with a more permissive wardrobe department
author: Dr. Priya Whelan
snippet: Hannibal is the lawman. Face is the saloon charmer. B.A. is the reluctant gunslinger. Murdock is the frontier trickster. The van is the horse. The client-of-the-week is the homesteader. Every episode is High Noon with a bulldozer.
full_text:
Hannibal is the lawman. Face is the saloon charmer. B.A. is the reluctant gunslinger. Murdock is the frontier trickster. The van is the horse. The client-of-the-week is the homesteader. Every episode is High Noon with a bulldozer.

When you teach students to watch for structure, The A-Team is a superb exhibit because the structure is not hidden under realism. It is right there, welded to the chassis. The show is not dumb. It is transparent. That is a rarer and harder thing. Dumb shows hide what they are. The A-Team tells you what it is in the opening credits and then does it.

title: TikTok served me a van-jump edit and now I'm six seasons deep
author: Noor H.
snippet: I thought this show would be boring in a boomer way and instead it is boring in a genius way. Nothing happens except the thing you wanted to happen. Every episode ends how it should end. I have not felt this calm watching TV since I was a small child.
full_text:
I thought this show would be boring in a boomer way and instead it is boring in a genius way. Nothing happens except the thing you wanted to happen. Every episode ends how it should end. I have not felt this calm watching TV since I was a small child.

My friends and I use 'I love it when a plan comes together' when any group project turns in on time. Face is objectively the hottest man in television history, do not argue with me on this. The ep where Murdock pretends to be a priest is better than any prestige TV I have paid for. This show is dumb in the way a cathedral is simple — on purpose, and perfectly.
